name: Slideshow
description: A simple presentation maker and presenter, unique per placement. Space editors build a deck of slides — stylized text (headings, body, bullet lists), uploaded images, and shapes — laid out freely on a fixed slide canvas; everything is positioned and sized proportionally so the deck looks identical from a 300×300 tile up to a scaled-up fullscreen presentation. Viewers can browse and present the deck but cannot edit it. A few show-wide settings (accent palette, background, slide aspect, and an optional "keep viewers in sync" mode that locks every viewer's presentation to the editor's current slide) sit alongside the slides. The whole deck lives in a single JSON file in a per-placement folder, with uploaded images (auto-resized to keep file sizes reasonable) stored beside it. No database or sync tables.
